title: How to Choose the Right SEO Tool in GoHighLevel — 2025
description: Compare GoHighLevel SEO tools vs. Ahrefs & SEMrush. Learn pricing, features, unlimited user seats, and which platform fits your agency in 2025.
html_content:
You're managing multiple client accounts. Your agency needs keyword tracking, rank monitoring, and SEO insights—fast. You've heard of Ahrefs and SEMrush, but they're expensive per user and don't integrate with your CRM. Then there's GoHighLevel, which bundles SEO tools directly into your all-in-one platform. But here's the question: Is GoHighLevel's built-in SEO functionality enough, or do you still need a standalone tool?

In this guide, I'll break down how GoHighLevel's SEO features stack against industry giants like Ahrefs and SEMrush—covering pricing, capabilities, user limits, and ROI for agencies. By the end, you'll know exactly which platform (or combination) makes sense for your business. And if you want to test GoHighLevel risk-free, this link gives you a free 30-day trial—double the standard 14-day offer.

📋 In This Guide

  • GoHighLevel SEO Features: What You Actually Get
  • Unlimited User Seats vs. Single-User Licensing: A Game-Changer for Agencies
  • Keyword Tracking & Historical Data: Where Each Platform Stands
  • Pricing Breakdown: ROI for Agencies Managing Multiple Clients
  • The Case for All-in-One: Why Agencies Stop Stacking Tools
  • How to Choose the Right Tool for Your Agency

GoHighLevel SEO Features: What You Actually Get

GoHighLevel's SEO toolkit isn't positioned as a replacement for Ahrefs or SEMrush—it's a complement to your CRM and marketing automation stack. Here's what's built in:

  • Keyword research & tracking: Monitor rank changes for tracked keywords across Google search results.
  • SEO-optimized content creation: AI-powered writing with built-in SEO suggestions, meta tag generation, and on-page optimization guidance.
  • Website audit tools: Crawl your own sites (or client sites) to flag technical SEO issues like broken links, missing meta tags, and mobile usability problems.
  • Backlink monitoring: Track referring domains and new backlinks to your site.
  • Search Atlas integration: Access to Search Atlas data (powered by Pixel Tools) for competitor analysis and keyword difficulty scoring.

The real advantage? These features live inside your GoHighLevel dashboard—no tab-switching, no separate login, no invoice to another vendor. For agencies running client campaigns through GHL workflows, that integration matters.

💡 Pro Tip

GoHighLevel's SEO tools shine when paired with its funnel builder and email automation. You can create an SEO-optimized landing page, track its rank in search, and automatically nurture leads from that page—all in one platform.

Unlimited User Seats vs. Single-User Licensing: A Game-Changer for Agencies

Here's where GoHighLevel fundamentally differs from Ahrefs and SEMrush.

Ahrefs pricing: Starts at $199/month for one user. If you need 5 team members accessing the same account, you're paying 5 × $199 = $995/month (or more if they need different permission levels).

SEMrush pricing: Starting at $120/month per user for their Business plan. Scale that to a team of 5, and you're looking at $600/month before add-ons.

GoHighLevel pricing plans: Standard plans range from $49–$499/month depending on features, and every seat is unlimited. Your entire agency team—10, 20, 50 people—can log in and use the same account simultaneously. No per-user cost. No overage fees.

For a 10-person agency, that's the difference between $2,000+/month on Ahrefs alone versus a flat $199–$499 on GoHighLevel's top tier.

Double the standard trial. No credit card required to start.

Keyword Tracking & Historical Data: Where Each Platform Stands

If your agency's bread and butter is ranking reports and historical performance data, this section matters.

Ahrefs: Offers unlimited keyword tracking with 2+ years of historical ranking data. The Rank Tracker tool is granular, allowing per-domain, per-keyword filtering, and detailed SERP history.

SEMrush: Position tracking includes up to 5,000 tracked keywords (depending on plan). Historical data goes back 12 months on most tiers.

GoHighLevel: Keyword tracking is available but more limited than Ahrefs. You can monitor rankings, but historical data retention and the number of tracked keywords depend on your subscription tier. It's sufficient for monitoring 20–100 keywords per client, but if you're tracking 500+ keywords across 10 clients, Ahrefs or SEMrush would be more robust.

Practical reality: Most small-to-medium agencies don't need unlimited keyword tracking. You track the 30–50 most important keywords per client and focus on ranking them. GoHighLevel covers this. If you need deep historical analysis and rank fluctuation trends, Ahrefs is worth the cost.

Pricing Breakdown: ROI for Agencies Managing Multiple Clients

Let's model a realistic scenario: a 5-person agency managing 10 client accounts.

Option A: Ahrefs only

  • Ahrefs: $199/month × 5 users = $995/month
  • CRM (HubSpot, Pipedrive, etc.): $150–$300/month
  • Total: $1,145–$1,295/month

Option B: SEMrush only

  • SEMrush: $120/month × 5 users = $600/month
  • CRM: $150–$300/month
  • Total: $750–$900/month

Option C: GoHighLevel (all-in-one)

  • GoHighLevel Pro (CRM + automation + SEO tools): $199/month
  • Add-ons/overages: $0 (unlimited users)
  • Total: $199/month

Over 12 months, GoHighLevel saves you $6,546–$13,152 compared to Ahrefs + separate CRM. That's your marketing budget, content creation costs, or additional team capacity.

💡 Pro Tip

The real ROI of GoHighLevel isn't just the SEO tools—it's eliminating manual data entry between platforms. When rank data syncs with your CRM, you spend less time on reporting and more time on strategy.

The Case for All-in-One: Why Agencies Stop Stacking Tools

Many agencies start with Ahrefs, add HubSpot for CRM, layer on Zapier for automation, and suddenly manage a 6-tool ecosystem. Each platform has its own login, billing, and data silos.

GoHighLevel consolidates this. Your SEO data lives in the same system as your lead pipeline, email sequences, and client automations. Benefits:

  • Faster reporting: Export rank data directly into client dashboards built in GoHighLevel.
  • Workflow automation: Trigger follow-ups when a client's target keyword hits the first page.
  • Unified billing: One invoice. One vendor relationship. Easier accounting.
  • Team adoption: New hires learn one platform, not five. Faster onboarding.

The tradeoff? GoHighLevel's SEO feature depth won't match specialized tools like Ahrefs. But for 80% of agencies, that 80% is sufficient—and the time saved is worth more than the marginal feature loss.

How to Choose the Right Tool for Your Agency

Ask yourself these questions:

1. How many keywords do I track per client?
If it's under 100 per client, GoHighLevel is fine. If it's 500+, use Ahrefs as your primary tool and GoHighLevel for integration.

2. Do I need competitor backlink analysis?
Ahrefs and SEMrush excel here. GoHighLevel covers basic backlink monitoring but isn't a competitor research powerhouse.

3. How important is team collaboration?
Unlimited user seats in GoHighLevel beat per-user pricing every time for teams over 3 people.

4. What's my primary business model?
If you're running client campaigns and funnels through GoHighLevel anyway, the integrated SEO tools are a natural extension. If you're a pure SEO agency, Ahrefs or SEMrush is your foundation.

Best practice hybrid: Use GoHighLevel as your agency operating system (CRM, funnels, automation, client reporting). Use Ahrefs or SEMrush for deep SEO research and competitive analysis. The two platforms complement each other without overlap.
